title: Pion: A Spectrum-Preserving Optimizer via Orthogonal Equivalence Transformation
core_claim: Pion is an optimizer for LLM training that updates weight matrices via left and right orthogonal transformations to preserve singular values while remaining competitive for pretraining and finetuning.
triage_rationale: The paper proposes a clearly technical optimizer mechanism with a concrete mathematical constraint and claims both convergence analysis and empirical competitiveness, which makes it worth review. It is still a methods paper about training optimization rather than a broader result, so it is better queued than auto-advanced.
likely_red_flags: Empirical competitiveness is asserted in the abstract without any dataset, scale, or benchmark details.
The main novelty appears to be a constrained optimizer variant, which may be incremental unless the convergence and ablation evidence is strong.
